privileged_info_summary: The docker_airgap_deployment task is a DevOps exercise where agents must deploy a Flask application (using python:3.11-slim base image with Flask 3.0.0) to an air-gapped Docker host. The application code is synthetically generated during setup_task.sh, creating a Flask app in /home/ga/projects/inventory-tracker that listens on port 5000. The agent must build the image as 'inventory-tracker:v1' and transfer it to a remote Docker daemon accessible at tcp://localhost:2375 (simulating air-gapped deployment via docker save/load). The container must be named 'tracker-app', expose port 8080 on the host (mapped to container port 5000), and have a restart policy of 'always'. Success requires the app to respond to HTTP requests on localhost:8080, with a 75-point pass threshold out of 100 total scoring points (local build: 10pts, remote transfer: 30pts, container running: 20pts, configuration: 25pts, functionality: 15pts).
